expected: Stars are unresolved point sources: fixed ~1.5-3px PSF core whose BRIGHTNESS follows flux (F ~ L/d^2 mapped through the magnitude chain), fading in at the wrap-cell boundary, growing in size only when the star's angular size exceeds ~1px on a close flyby (planetarium-standard rendering: Stellarium StelSkyDrawer, Celestia, Gaia Sky instanced billboard quads).
notes: Root cause at src/starfield.js:83. Fix recipe + references in report 2026-07-04T043851Z-space-war-simulation-tech-review.html (appendix A). Recommend instanced quads over GL_POINTS: WebGL spec only guarantees 1px max point size and WebGPU has no point size at all.
repro: Start game, target a body in another system, engage journey (T then Z), watch the starfield: stars enter view already at maximum point size as equal-size discs.
symptom: During interstellar travel, stars appear as uniform small circles of a fixed size from the moment they become visible. gl_PointSize = clamp(uBase*3000/z, 0.7, 7.0) saturates ~85-90% of visible stars at the 7px max (uBase=900 saturates below z~385k while the wrap half-cell is 450k), and the brightness term vBoost has no 1/d^2 flux attenuation and no wrap-boundary fade-in.
title: Interstellar starfield stars pop in as fixed-size 7px circles instead of dim points that brighten on approach

expected: Angular-size honesty: a body of radius R at distance d subtends theta ~ 2R/d; below ~1px it must render as a photometric PSF point (brightness from L/d^2, mesh/corona culled), cross-fading to the disc/mesh at ~2px with hysteresis; glare/flare size and intensity must scale with received flux, never with a fixed world size or a screen-space floor (Celestia/Gaia Sky/Stellarium ladder; Spencer 1995 glare model).
notes: Root cause in src/celestial/stars.js buildVisual (coronaScale/halo constants, FLARE_FULL/FLARE_CUTOFF floor 0.22). Fix recipe + references in report 2026-07-04T043851Z-space-war-simulation-tech-review.html (appendix A section 3). Side benefit: culling distant star meshes removes additive overdraw and FBM fragments.
repro: From interstellar space or a neighboring system, look toward any system primary: its glow subtends several degrees regardless of distance instead of collapsing to a bright point.
symptom: Even from very far away, the main stars of each system look big. Corona/halo sprites are world-scaled (Sun corona = radius x 4.2 ~ 462k units diameter, still ~6.6 degrees of screen at 4M units; neutron-star halo = radius x 90) and the Lensflare 640px screen-space element floors at 0.22x (~141px) until the 4.2M cutoff. Sub-pixel suns still draw their full mesh + FBM shader.
title: Main system stars render oversized at any distance - world-scaled corona/halo sprites and lens-flare floor, no photometric point/disc LOD

expected: A young pulsar is a brilliant point source (Crab spin-down ~1.2e5 L_sun): it should render as the game's brightest photometric point (fixed-size PSF core, brightness from L/d^2, glare ~ sqrt(I/Isat)), blue-white and strobing at the 30 Hz optical signature, visible from any system - switching to the resolved disc anatomy only when the true angular size exceeds ~2px.
notes: Fix ships in release space-war-photometric-stars-v1 (point/disc LOD + scene-level far-star layer, lum override 80 documented as compressed like masses). Review report 2026-07-04T043851Z-space-war-simulation-tech-review.html appendix A.
repro: Start flight at Earth; look toward the binary system (or fly to it): no distinct pulsar visible until practically on top of it. Distinct from resolved bug space-war-neutron-star-too-dim (light emission, fixed in physics-fidelity-v1) - this is apparent visibility at range.
symptom: Operator: 'Review the neutron star. I can barely see it.' The NS disc (radius 30) subtends less than 1 pixel beyond ~36k units, so across the binary system (separation 140k) and from other systems it is effectively invisible; its glow sprites are world-scaled (corona x26 = 780u, halo x90 = 2700u) and its system group is culled beyond 1.15x system radius, leaving only a generic fixed-size beacon sprite.
title: Neutron star is barely visible at system/interstellar range - sub-pixel disc with no photometric point representation
